title: try / catch / finally: формы и гарантии

cards.prompt: try adds T and throws, catch adds C, finally runs when control leaves the try block and adds F; the order is TCF
cards.code: using System; string s=""; try { s+="T"; throw new InvalidOperationException(); } catch (Exception) { s+="C"; } finally { s+="F"; } Console.WriteLine(s);

cards.prompt: return is a jump statement, so finally runs when control leaves the try block before the value is handed back; then 1 is returned, printing try;finally;r=1
cards.code: using System; string log=""; int M(){ try { log+="try;"; return 1; } finally { log+="finally;"; } } int r=M(); Console.WriteLine(log+"r="+r);

cards.prompt: catch clauses are checked top to bottom and at most one runs; ArgumentNullException matches first, so s is argnull even though ArgumentException and Exception would also match
cards.code: using System; string s=""; try { throw new ArgumentNullException("p"); } catch (ArgumentNullException) { s="argnull"; } catch (ArgumentException) { s="arg"; } catch (Exception) { s="exc"; } Console.WriteLine(s);
